summary: Drew DeVault announces he is stepping back from blogging after nine years and 300,000 words. He expresses fatigue from diminishing returns on his writing—less discussion, unchanged industry problems, and a steady stream of hostile responses. He references Bryan Cantrill's metaphor about Oracle as a lawnmower without empathy, extending it to argue the entire tech industry shares that character. He commits to continuing his work quietly, building what he believes in, while leaving a curated list of his most valued posts as a farewell.
key_points: After nine years and 300,000 words of blogging, DeVault feels he has exhausted what he has to say on his core topics
The returns on writing have diminished—less reader engagement and discussion—while the costs of hostile responses remain constant
The software industry remains as broken as when he started, and perverse incentives make advocacy feel futile
He extends Cantrill's Oracle-as-lawnmower metaphor to indict the entire industry as lacking empathy
He will continue building software aligned with his principles but without public commentary for the foreseeable future
money_quotes: Over the last nine years I have written 300,000 words for this blog on the topics which are important to me. I am not certain that I have much left to say.
Each new article spurs less discussion than the last, but provides an unwavering supply of spiteful responses.
Software is still the same mess it was when I started writing and working, or perhaps even worse.
The truth he did not speak is that we all have some Oracle in our hearts, and the lawnmower is the size of the entire industry.
I will continue my work quietly, building the things I believe in, and remaining true to my principles.
I do not yet know if this is a cessation or a siesta, but I do know that I will not write again for some time.
key_insight: After years of principled advocacy, DeVault concludes that writing cannot overcome the tech industry's perverse incentives, and chooses to let his work speak for itself in silence.
